risks: SQL MI General Purpose tier does not support zone redundancy; a single-zone failure could impact database availability while the presentation and app tiers are zone-redundant
No diagnostic settings resources linking VMSS, Load Balancer, NSGs, or SQL MI to the Log Analytics workspace — monitoring will not be functional out of the box
Key Vault has publicNetworkAccess set to Disabled with no private endpoint defined; SQL MI managed identity may be unable to reach the vault for TDE CMK key operations
No Key Vault RBAC role assignment resource granting the SQL MI managed identity the Key Vault Crypto Service Encryption User role required for TDE CMK
Application-tier VMSS (vmss-app-prod-001) has no autoscale configuration and runs at fixed capacity of 2
No resource tagging strategy defined across resources for cost tracking, environment identification, or operational management
correctionsRecommended: Add a Key Vault private endpoint in the SQL MI subnet (or a VNet service endpoint) to allow the managed identity to access TDE keys while publicNetworkAccess remains Disabled
Add a Microsoft.Authorization/roleAssignments resource granting id-sqlmi-prod-001 the Key Vault Crypto Service Encryption User role on kv-threetier-prod-001
Add Microsoft.Insights/diagnosticSettings resources for vmss-presentation-prod-001, vmss-app-prod-001, lbe-presentation-prod-001, and sqlmi-threetier-prod-001 targeting log-threetier-prod-001
Consider adding a Microsoft.Insights/autoscaleSettings resource for vmss-app-prod-001 to handle variable application-tier load
Add a consistent tagging convention (e.g., environment, costCenter, owner) to all resource definitions
Evaluate upgrading SQL MI to Business Critical tier if zone redundancy is required for the database tier
hardDependencies: Key Vault RBAC role assignment for id-sqlmi-prod-001 must exist before SQL MI can configure TDE with customer-managed key
